title: Forward Deployed Sustainability Engineer, Sustainability AI
company_focus:
Enterprise sustainability platform for climate and ESG data
management
description:
About Watershed

Watershed is the enterprise sustainability platform.
Companies like Airbnb, Carlyle Group, FedEx, Visa, and Dr.
Martens use Watershed to manage climate and ESG data,
produce audit-ready metrics for voluntary and regulatory
reporting including CSRD, and drive real decarbonization. We
are looking for team members who love product-building, want
to work hard at a mission-oriented startup, and will
collaborate with us in shaping the culture of a growing
team.

We have offices in San Francisco, New York, Denver, London,
Paris, Berlin, Sydney, Mexico City, and remote team members
across the US and Europe. We hope that you'll be interested
in joining us!

The role

Forward Deployed Sustainability Engineers (FDSEs) are a new
kind of role at Watershed: part technical builder, part
trusted customer partner, part product iterator. You'll sit
at the intersection of our product engineering teams and our
customers, doing the hands-on, creative work of figuring out
how Watershed's newest AI products deliver outcomes for
sustainability teams in the real world.

This is a deliberately experimental role. We're building a
small incubation team that embeds directly within our
product and engineering teams and works closely alongside
our customers on our new AI products. You'll co-develop AI
skills, build prototypes, agents and workflows, and bring
back fast, high-quality feedback to shape how our products
evolve.

Concretely, you'll focus on one of Watershed's flagship AI
products for the core measure, report, and act loop. You’ll
work closely with our largest, most strategic customers to
drive real scope 3 decarbonization by using Product
Footprints.

What You'll Do

- Embed directly with product and engineering teams and
  customers to drive adoption and success through beta, and
  GA stages
- Co-develop agent skills, prompts, and workflows that unlock
  new use cases and help customers bring more of their
  sustainability work into Watershed
- Build AI-assisted scripts and prototypes that solve novel
  sustainability problems and demonstrate their value for
  customers
- Work directly alongside our account teams and our customers:
  joining calls, embedding in their workflows, running
  experiments, and building trust as a partner
- Close the feedback loop between customers and product by
  identifying what's working, what isn't, and what to build
  next
- Partner with Customer Org leaders to package use cases,
  learnings, and playbooks so that what works in the field
  can scale across customer-facing teams

What We're Looking For

You don't need to be a perfect fit across every dimension,
but you should have strong demonstrated aptitude or hands-on
experience in most of these areas:

Technical:

- You've built things: scripts, prototypes, data pipelines, or
  AI-assisted workflows. You're comfortable writing
  prototype quality code and aren't afraid to move fast.
- Experience with AI tooling, including building skills,
  building products with LLMs, or prototyping agent
  workflows
- Fluent with data tools (Excel, SQL, Python): wrangling messy
  data, building lightweight pipelines, turning raw inputs
  into usable outputs

Sustainability:

- You understand corporate sustainability: scope 1/2/3
  emissions, product footprints, supply chain
  decarbonization, or related domains

Customer-facing:

- You've worked directly with customers or technical
  stakeholders to build trust and solve complex problems
- You can tell a compelling story with data, from raw numbers
  to insight to recommendation

Prior Experience:

- Minimum of 6 years in a sustainability, technical or
  customer-facing role, ideally all 3
- You're energized by ambiguity and fast iteration. This is an
  incubation team and things will change.
- You connect the dots between technical possibilities and
  real-world impact





Must be willing to work from an office 4 days per week
(except for remote roles)

Watershed has hub offices in San Francisco, New York,
London, and Mexico City and satellite offices in Denver,
Sydney, Paris, and Berlin. Where we have offices, employees
are expected to be in office for 4 days per week. Certain
jobs are open to being remote and will be specifically noted
on the jobs page and in the job description if so.

What’s the interview process like?

It starts the same for every candidate: getting to know the
team members through 1 to 2 conversations about Watershed,
your experience, and your interests. Next steps can vary by
role, but usual next steps are a skill or experience
interview (e.g. a coding interview for an engineer, a
portfolio review for a designer, deeper experience call for
other roles) which leads to a virtual or in person interview
panel. We prioritize transparency and lack of surprise
throughout the process.

What if I need accommodations for my interview?

At Watershed, we are dedicated to ensuring an inclusive
recruitment process. We provide reasonable accommodations
for candidates with disabilities, long-term conditions,
mental health needs, religious observances, neurodivergence,
or pregnancy-related support requirements. If you need
assistance during your process, please contact your
recruiter.
responsibilities: Embed directly with product and engineering teams and customers to drive adoption through beta and GA stages
Co-develop agent skills, prompts, and workflows that unlock new sustainability use cases
Build AI-assisted scripts and prototypes that solve novel sustainability problems for customers
Work directly with customers by joining calls, embedding in workflows, and running experiments
Close the feedback loop between customers and product by identifying what works and what to build next
Partner with Customer Org leaders to package use cases and playbooks for scaling across teams
use_cases: Scope 3 decarbonization through Product Footprints using AI agents
Automated sustainability data management and reporting (CSRD, ESG)
Agent-driven workflows for corporate sustainability measurement and action
AI-assisted sustainability problem solving and prototyping
Supply chain emissions tracking and decarbonization planning
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building and deploying AI
systems — specifically co-developing agent skills, prompts,
and workflows, building AI-assisted scripts and prototypes,
and working hands-on with LLMs and agent workflows for
Watershed's AI products. While customer-facing, the FDSE is
actively constructing and iterating on AI agents and
workflows, making this ai-first.
